content:
# Safaniya Member

**Summary**: A Middle Cretaceous carbonate and clastic unit within the Wasia Formation.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1997-al-qassab|Al-Qassab, 1997]] studied the Safaniya Member to characterize its reservoir properties and heterogeneity.

- [[2013-atig|atig, 2013]] studied the Safaniya member to identify and predict sand body geometry.

## Depositional Environment

- [[1997-al-qassab|Al-Qassab, 1997]] fluvial to fluvial dominated delta

- [[2013-atig|atig, 2013]] studied the Safaniya member to identify and predict sand body geometry.

## Reservoir Properties

- [[1997-al-qassab|Al-Qassab, 1997]] porous and permeable sandstone; quality deteriorates moving upward

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The Safaniya Member is characterized by the absence of major conglomeratic facies except at channel lags. Clean sandstone units are abundant... These clean sandstone units constitute the Safaniya Reservoir. ([[1997-al-qassab|Al-Qassab, 1997, p. 25]])

- The Safaniya Member includes the Lower Safaniya Stringers, the Safaniya Main Sands, and the Upper Safaniya Stringers. ([[2013-atig|atig, 2013, p. 10]])
